---
title: "MIN HE"
page-layout: full
image: images/hemin_2026.jpg
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 20em
  image-shape: rectangle
  links:
    - icon: mailbox
      text: Reach me per email
      href: mailto:MHE009@e.ntu.edu.sg
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/qiyuanlanniao
format:
  html:
    link-external-newwindow: true
---

::: {#hero-heading}

My research interests lie in multimodal, large models, machine learning, visual language navigation, and embodied intelligence.

## Background

- 2026-Present: Master of Science, Nanyang Technological University, specializing in Machine Learning, under supervision of Prof. [Lihua Xie](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Fmrv3J8AAAAJ&hl=en).

- 2021-2025: Bachelor of Engineering (Honours), Chongqing University of Technology, majoring in Computer Science and Technology, under supervision of Prof. [Xiaofei Zhu](https://www.atailab.cn/).

## Academic Experience

::: {.project-header}
[OmniVLN: Open-Set Instance-Level 3D Semantic Mapping with Rotating LiDAR and Panoramic Vision]{.project-title}
[Under Research]{.project-date}
:::
::: {.project-desc}
To address the issues of missing spatial logic and context overload in large-scale complex scenarios, this method constructs a dynamic scene graph and a multi-resolution attention model, achieving omnidirectional consistent 3D semantic mapping with a long-range navigation success rate of 87.56%. Furthermore, it reduces token costs by up to 56% through backend geometry pre-computation.
:::

::: {.project-header}
[HSM-Net: A Multimodal Diagnostic Framework for Abdominal Trauma Based on Frequency Domain Denoising and LLM Prior]{.project-title}
[Under Research]{.project-date}
:::
::: {.project-desc}
To address the challenges of speckle noise interference and the multimodal semantic gap in the diagnosis of abdominal trauma ultrasound, this framework innovatively integrates FFT frequency domain denoising, LLM semantic injection, and a dynamic MoE expert system, achieving an accuracy of 91.87%, comprehensively surpassing 12 state-of-the-art methods such as FedMedCLIP and CardiacCLIP.
:::

::: {.project-header}
[A Review of Data Fusion and Deep Learning Models for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis]{.project-title}
[Completed]{.project-date}
:::
::: {.project-desc}
He, M. (2025). A Review of Data Fusion and Deep Learning Models for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis. In Proceedings of the 1st International Conference on E-commerce and Artificial Intelligence - **ECAI**; ISBN 978-989-758-726-9, SciTePress, pages 5-12. DOI: 10.5220/0013189700004568
:::

## Project Experience

::: {.project-header}
<div>
  [AI Agent Intelligent Application Development]{.project-title}
</div>
[2024.07 - 2024.09]{.project-date}
:::
- Led the design and core development of a multi-functional AI Agent application; conducted requirement analysis, technology selection, and architecture design, implementing core AI logic for intent recognition and task decomposition using LLM and LangChain.
- Independently developed the RAG module, building a specialized knowledge base and optimizing semantic search; enabled dynamic tool scheduling through ToolKit, empowering the agent to autonomously perform complex tasks.
- Designed and implemented a multi-turn conversation memory system and explored emotion detection. Integrated speech synthesis and Telegram Bot, and built an API layer using FastAPI to facilitate front-end and third-party system integration.
- Led the Docker deployment and Langsmith monitoring; overcame key technical challenges, including custom tool integration, memory persistence, and multi-tool parameter validation, ensuring smooth engineering and operational performance.


## Honors & Awards
- Outstanding graduates of Chongqing, 2025
- Outstanding Bachelor's Thesis Award, 2025
- National Scholarship, 2024
- Second Prize in the National Undergraduate Data Statistics and Analysis Competition, 2023
- Finalist Award in the CCF National Algorithm Elite Competition, 2023
- Third Prize in the National Finals of the Team Programming Ladder Competition, 2023
- First Prize in the National Undergraduate Mathematics Competition, 2023
- Second Prize in the National Undergraduate Mathematical Modeling Competition, 2023

:::
